jokes.text: You sure you're not just being humble? (Alex's sarcastic response to Bobby's claim he got the job for being good-looking)
jokes.setup: Bobby earnestly explains he got a suntan lotion commercial because he's good-looking
jokes.punchline: Alex sarcastically questions if Bobby is being humble about his looks
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted sarcasm that reveals Alex's character as the straight-man who deflates Bobby's ego with perfect timing

jokes.text: What's a kebel? / It take 270 kebel make a lifnitsch
jokes.setup: Louie questions Latka's foreign currency/measurement system
jokes.punchline: Latka's nonsensical conversion ratios that go nowhere
jokes.explanation: Classic Latka absurdist humor building through escalating nonsensical measurements

jokes.text: What's a matta? / I don't know nothing. What's a matta with you?
jokes.setup: Louie continues asking about Latka's made-up currency system
jokes.punchline: Latka reveals it was all nonsense from a bubble gum wrapper and turns it into 'What's the matter with you?'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection and wordplay that subverts the entire elaborate setup with a simple pun. Peak Latka character work.

jokes.text: I was just pulling your yaktabe
jokes.setup: Latka apologizes for the prank
jokes.punchline: Uses another made-up foreign phrase that sounds like 'pulling your leg'
jokes.explanation: Nice follow-up that maintains Latka's character voice while being a subtle callback to the prank

jokes.text: Get lost, yokel. / God, I love New York.
jokes.setup: Louie rudely dismisses the newcomer Steve
jokes.punchline: Steve's naive positive response to being insulted
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing naive newcomer misinterpreting hostility as authentic New York charm

jokes.text: What are the chances of that? / 50-50? / At least.
jokes.setup: Tony marvels at the coincidence of meeting an actor in NYC
jokes.punchline: Alex's deadpan acknowledgment that it's incredibly common
jokes.explanation: Understated humor about NYC being full of aspiring actors, delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: I play the only guy at a party without a tan
jokes.setup: Bobby explains his 'challenging' commercial role
jokes.punchline: The role is absurdly simple and shallow
jokes.explanation: Solid joke about the banality of commercial work, fits Bobby's character arc

jokes.text: If it's 'brafla,' I have to kill a man. / I think it's 'bratla.' / Oh, good, good. Now I won't need the rest of the day off.
jokes.setup: Latka can't read Louie's handwriting on a message
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of murder based on mispronunciation, treating it like a scheduling issue
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark absurdist humor with perfect escalation and casual delivery of the punchline about not needing time off

jokes.text: I leaned out of the cab to tell this guy he was an idiot and I forgot to roll the window down
jokes.setup: Tony's face is swollen from an accident
jokes.punchline: He injured himself while calling someone else an idiot
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy with perfect irony - the guy calling someone an idiot proves himself one. Great character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Is this good luck charm in your country? / No. / Then, uh, why are you giving it to me? / I don't want it.
jokes.setup: Latka gives Bobby goat droppings for his commercial
jokes.punchline: It's not a good luck charm, he just doesn't want it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Latka logic delivered with deadpan honesty. The buildup and deflation is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: He doesn't respect my leadership. / What are you talking about? He's like everyone else here. He fears you. He pays you bribes. He hates you.
jokes.setup: Louie complains Bobby doesn't respect his leadership
jokes.punchline: Alex matter-of-factly lists how Bobby actually does treat Louie like everyone else
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy that efficiently summarizes the power dynamic while being brutally honest

jokes.text: Louie, why would anyone go out of his way to deliver awful, depressing news to another human being? / Kicks.
jokes.setup: Alex asks why Louie wants to deliver bad news to Bobby
jokes.punchline: Louie's simple, honest answer: 'Kicks'
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character integration - one word that completely captures Louie's sadistic nature

jokes.text: Make it one word. / Is 'super-loser' one word?
jokes.setup: Negotiating how few words Louie can use to tell Bobby the bad news
jokes.punchline: Louie's creative interpretation of 'one word'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the negotiation with Louie's typical creative cruelty

jokes.text: No time for love. Stop that man!
jokes.setup: Louie is being physically restrained from calling Bobby
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration while being tackled
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment with Louie's theatrical overreaction to being stopped

jokes.text: He's losing his pants. / Don't you wish.
jokes.setup: Comment about Louie losing his pants in the struggle
jokes.punchline: Alex's dry response suggesting they'd enjoy that
jokes.explanation: Minor visual gag callback with typical Alex dry delivery

jokes.text: Hi. I'd like to send a singing telegram. / Louie!
jokes.setup: Louie promises not to call Bobby
jokes.punchline: Immediately tries a different method to deliver the bad news
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Louie's persistence and creativity in finding loopholes

jokes.text: What's the matter with your face? / Tony, please. / Well, look at his face.
jokes.setup: Tony keeps commenting on Bobby's suntan makeup
jokes.punchline: His persistence despite being told to stop
jokes.explanation: Running gag that builds through Tony's tactless persistence

jokes.text: They couldn't pay me enough to screw up my face like that.
jokes.setup: Tony continues commenting on Bobby's makeup
jokes.punchline: The irony given Tony's own swollen face from his window accident
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic callback to Tony's earlier accident, showing his complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: I guess I'm gonna have to tell everybody that my father's side of the family comes from Florida
jokes.setup: Bobby's face is half-tanned from the commercial
jokes.punchline: His creative explanation for the uneven tan
jokes.explanation: Clever character moment showing Bobby trying to make the best of his situation

jokes.text: A fine, understanding, easy-going, level-headed, roll-with-the-punches kind of guy
jokes.setup: Alex's toast to Bobby as they prepare to tell him bad news
jokes.punchline: The ironic listing of qualities they hope Bobby will display
jokes.explanation: Setup for the upcoming reveal with dramatic irony as audience knows what's coming

jokes.text: Yeah, well, you know, if you like revivals
jokes.setup: Steve announces he's playing Romeo in Romeo and Juliet
jokes.punchline: Alex's attempt to downplay the significance of a classic play
jokes.explanation: Good attempt at minimizing Steve's achievement to soften the blow to Bobby

jokes.text: What the hell are you laughing at? / Oh, he's laughing at my face. Pretty funny, huh? / I haven't even gotten to your face yet.
jokes.setup: Bobby thinks Louie is laughing at his appearance
jokes.punchline: Louie reveals he has other reasons to mock Bobby first
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie cruelty with good escalation - his mockery runs deeper than just appearance

jokes.text: I'm not an actor anymore... Since I decided it was stupid
jokes.setup: Bobby just got the role of Romeo
jokes.punchline: He's decided to quit acting the same day he got his dream role
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Bobby's terrible timing creates painful comedy

jokes.text: And it doesn't have to be anything nice. Just whatever you got lying around. / Oh, gee, I wanted to give you a nice one.
jokes.setup: Bobby humbly asks for any cab since he's given up acting
jokes.punchline: Louie's mock disappointment at not being able to give him something worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie character moment - even his fake kindness is cruel

jokes.text: Bobby, I appreciate your help, but could you give me a little more? / Actors.
jokes.setup: Steve asks Bobby to put more effort into rehearsing
jokes.punchline: Bobby's dismissive response despite being an actor himself
jokes.explanation: Good character irony with Bobby dismissing actors while being one himself

jokes.text: I am... s-s-slain.
jokes.setup: Bobby performing Romeo's death scene
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top dramatic delivery despite claiming he's done with acting
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Bobby's natural acting ability despite his cynicism

jokes.text: [Long pause after Romeo's death scene as everyone processes Bobby's talent]
jokes.setup: Bobby delivers a powerful Romeo death scene
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable silence as everyone realizes how talented he is
jokes.explanation: Powerful use of silence as comedy - the awkward beat speaks volumes about Bobby's wasted talent

jokes.text: Ho, this day just keeps getting better by the minute. / Worked like a charm, didn't it, Rieger?
jokes.setup: Louie's sadistic pleasure at the situation
jokes.punchline: His gloating about the plan working to show Bobby's talent
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie enjoying others' emotional pain, delivered with characteristic glee

jokes.text: I wish I had this on film! Thank you, Lord! Thank you.
jokes.setup: Louie continues to enjoy Bobby's internal conflict
jokes.punchline: His theatrical gratitude to the heavens for this entertainment
jokes.explanation: Escalation of Louie's sadistic pleasure with over-the-top religious thanks

jokes.text: That light is you, Lou. You're my inspiration... maybe one day I might be able to come back here in this garage and stuff every laugh you've ever had at my expense right down your throat.
jokes.setup: Bobby's seemingly heartfelt speech about finding inspiration
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Louie's cruelty is his motivation for revenge
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection speech that completely subverts expectations. Starts as inspiration, ends as revenge fantasy. Peak character writing.

jokes.text: Thank you, Louie. / You're welcome.
jokes.setup: Bobby thanks Louie after threatening him
jokes.punchline: Louie's automatic polite response before processing what happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect button to the scene - Louie's conditioned politeness trumps his comprehension

jokes.text: Well, Louie, the Lord giveth, and the Lord taketh away.
jokes.setup: Louie's moment of triumph has been completely undermined
jokes.punchline: Alex's biblical reference to Louie's emotional whiplash
jokes.explanation: Nice capper to the episode with Alex's wry observation about Louie's reversal of fortune
